spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (document.querySelector, LanguageDetector, detect() with runtime script (~6912 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (2 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (appropriate, confidence, content, detect, device, handler, handlers, identifies, language, languagedetector, locale, mat).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (4 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("A JavaScript API for detecting the language of text, with confidence levels.  An important supplement to translation is language detection. This can be combined with translation, e.g. taking user input in an unknown language and translating") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://webmachinelearning.github.io/translation-api/#language-detector-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~6912 chars of script across 2 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 3 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
